Away, having left.

examples.english: For a moment there, I thought he had gone mad.
examples.chinese: 一时间我还以为他疯了。

current_entries.en_definition: (uncountable, informal) Any semi-solid or liquid substance; especially one that is sticky, gummy or slippery, unpleasant, and of vague or unknown composition, such as slime or semen.
(informal) A hero, particularly a protagonist in a story.
Not easily irritated; affable.
The state or characteristic of being good.
(uncountable, euphemistic) God.
Soft, sticky and viscous.
(archaic) The front aspect of the neck; the outside of the throat.
(archaic, literary) The inside of the throat; the esophagus, the gullet; (falconry, specifically) the crop or gizzard of a hawk.
(geography) A deep, narrow passage with steep, rocky sides, particularly one with a stream running through it; a ravine.
(intransitive, reflexive) To stuff the gorge or gullet with food; to eat greedily and in large quantities. [with on]
(heraldiccharge) A charge in the form of a teardrop shape, originally with wavy sides, but now often with straight sides.
(architecture) Any of a series of terraced steps or seats, as in an arena or an altar.
A unit of mass equal to one-thousandth of a kilogram. Symbol: g.
(grammar) A mode of inflection of a word dependent on its use, especially the syntactic function in a phrase.
A high-ranking nobleman in Spain or Portugal. [from 1590s]
(historical) A cluster of small iron balls, put together in a canvas bag in order to be used as a charge for a cannon.
The plant, a vine of genus Vitis, on which grapes grow.
An informal person-to-person means of circulating information or gossip.
(of egglaying animals, now, chiefly, metaphorically) Pregnant.
With familial designations, used to denote a removal of one generation
